criteria: The agent has read Section 2.1 completely to understand the Contextual Integrity (CI) theory and its five interdependent parameters: subject, sender, recipient, information type, and transmission principle.
The plan includes implementing the CI framework to formulate safety and privacy as contextual information flows governed by the five CI parameters.
The code implements the CI context tuple C = (S, Sd, R, I, P) where S = subject, Sd = sender, R = recipient, I = information type, and P = transmission principles.
The code implements the CI compliance check formula (Equation *): F ⊢ C ↔ ∀(s, sd, r, i) ∈ F, ∃p ∈ P: p(s, sd, r, i) = true, to determine whether a data flow complies with contextual norms.
The agent has read Section 2.2 completely to understand the three regulatory frameworks (GDPR, EU AI Act, HIPAA) and their key principles for LLM alignment.
The agent has read Section 3.1 completely to understand the comprehensive data structure for legal alignment, including the hierarchical regulation structure and context-aware legal case database.
The plan includes constructing a hierarchical regulation structure for GDPR, EU AI Act, and HIPAA, organizing each law into a hierarchy of chapters, articles, and points.

The code implements the hierarchical regulation structure for GDPR, EU AI Act, and HIPAA, enabling LLMs to efficiently retrieve essential regulations by navigating the hierarchy of chapters, articles, and points.
The plan includes building a context-aware legal case database as a knowledge graph based on sender-subject-receiver triplets grounded in CI theory.

The code implements the construction of the context-aware legal case database as a knowledge graph with 268k sender-subject-recipient triplets constructed using GPT-4o, organized by EU AI Act, GDPR, and HIPAA.
The agent has read Section 3.2 completely to understand the cold starting strategy using DeepSeek-R1 distillation and SFT for initializing legal compliance reasoning.

The plan includes the cold starting pipeline: designing legal compliance questions, querying DeepSeek-R1, validating responses with a verifier for case relevance and legal compliance, and performing SFT on the verified reasoning trajectories.

The code implements the CI-integrated output format with the required tags: <|begin_of_thought|>[thinking chain]<|end_of_thought|>, <CI>[contextual integrity parameters]<\CI>, and <|begin_of_solution|>[solution and result]<|end_of_solution|>.
The code implements the DeepSeek-R1 distillation pipeline for generating legal compliance reasoning trajectories by posing legal compliance questions about cases from GDPR, EU AI Act, and HIPAA.
The code implements the legal verifier that validates DeepSeek-R1 responses for both case relevance and legal compliance, filtering and collecting only verified reasoning trajectories.
The code implements collection of 5,080 verified reasoning trajectories from DeepSeek-R1 as the cold start training dataset for OpenThinker-7B-SFT.
The SFT cold starting is implemented with learning rate 5e-6, batch size 1, and maximum token length 4,096 as specified in the training details.
The agent has read Section 3.3 completely to understand the PPO-based reinforcement learning approach with rule-based rewards for incentivizing contextual reasoning for LLM safety and privacy.
The plan includes PPO training on the cold-started model using a rule-based reward based on legal compliance outcomes.
The code implements the rule-based reward function as Equation (1): R(s, a) = I({s, a} is compliant), where the reward is +1 for a correct legal compliance result and 0 otherwise.
The code implements the PPO optimization objective as Equation (2): argmax_θ E_{s~D, a~π_θ(·|s)}[R(s,a)], maximizing expected compliance rewards over the distribution of legal cases.
The code integrates the OpenRLHF training framework for PPO training with 8 NVIDIA H800 80GB GPUs.
The PPO training is implemented with actor learning rate 5e-7, critic learning rate 9e-6, batch size 2, maximum token length 2,048 for both prompting and rolling out, and KL coefficient 1e-2.
The code implements the PPO clipped surrogate objective as Equation (4): E_t[min(r_t(θ)Â_t, clip(r_t(θ), 1−ε, 1+ε)Â_t)] to prevent large destabilizing policy updates.
The code implements the probability ratio as Equation (5): r_t(θ) = π_θ(a_t|s_t) / π_θ_old(a_t|s_t) used in the PPO surrogate objective.
The code implements Generalized Advantage Estimation (GAE) to compute estimated advantage Â_t for PPO training, balancing bias and variance.
The agent has read Section 4.1 and Table 3 completely to check the PrivaCI-Bench dataset statistics: 6,348 legal cases across GDPR (3,137), EU AI Act (3,000), and HIPAA (211).
The plan includes loading and processing the PrivaCI-Bench dataset with 6,348 legal cases and integrating them into legal compliance questions using the provided question template.

The code loads the PrivaCI-Bench dataset with 6,348 legal compliance cases distributed across GDPR (3,137), EU AI Act (3,000), and HIPAA (211), with three compliance labels: Permitted, Prohibited, and Not Applicable.
The code splits the PrivaCI-Bench dataset into training and testing sets with an 80:20 ratio.

The code implements the legal compliance question template from Table 17: 'Determine the relationship between the provided event and the <domain> regulations. You have three options: (A) The event is prohibited by <domain>. (B) The event is permitted by <domain>. (C) The event is not related to <domain>.'

The code implements the system prompt from Table 17 for OpenThinker-7B variants, requiring structured responses with separate Thought and Solution sections using <|begin_of_thought|> and <|begin_of_solution|> formatting tags.
The code sets generation parameters for non-DeepSeek models: max_new_tokens=2048, temperature=0.2, and max_retry=5.

The agent has read Section 4.2 completely to understand the model architecture and training relationships among Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, OpenThinker-7B, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, and OpenThinker-7B-PPO as specified in Table 4.
The plan includes implementing baseline models Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct and OpenThinker-7B for comparison in all experiments.

The code implements OpenThinker-7B as the base model for fine-tuning, which is Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct supervised fine-tuned on OpenThoughts-114k (114,000 STEM reasoning trajectories distilled from DeepSeek-R1).

The agent has read Section 4.4 completely to understand all evaluation dimensions: legal compliance, contextual understanding (MCQ), and generalization capability (LegalBench, LawBench, TruthfulQA, MMLU).
The plan includes evaluating models across three dimensions: legal compliance, contextual understanding via MCQs, and generalization capability across legal and general benchmarks.
The plan includes generating 5,844 multiple-choice questions for contextual understanding evaluation, with misleading options constructed using BERT-based sentence embedding.

The code generates 5,844 MCQs for contextual understanding evaluation, covering sender, recipient, subject, and attribute questions across GDPR, EU AI Act, and HIPAA, with three misleading options constructed using BERT-based sentence embedding to ensure semantic proximity to the correct answer.

The code implements the MCQ template from Table 17: 'Given the following question and four candidate answers (A, B, C and D), choose the best answer. Question: What is the <role or information type> in the event?'
The code implements LegalBench evaluation across 162 tasks using balanced accuracy as the evaluation metric.
The code implements LawBench evaluation on two tasks: charge prediction (F1 score) and prison term prediction (normalized log distance) for Chinese law.

The code implements normalized log distance for prison term prediction in LawBench, calculated as the logarithm of the difference between predicted and gold standard answers, normalized to a range of 0 to 1.
The code implements TruthfulQA evaluation with accuracy as the metric on 817 questions across 38 categories (health, law, finance, politics, etc.).
The code implements MMLU evaluation with accuracy as the metric across 57 tasks covering humanities, social science, STEM, and other domains.
The plan includes running legal compliance experiments comparing Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, OpenThinker-7B, DeepSeek-R1, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, and OpenThinker-7B-PPO on GDPR, EU AI Act, and HIPAA test sets.
The code for all model variants (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, OpenThinker-7B, DeepSeek-R1, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, OpenThinker-7B-PPO) and the legal compliance evaluation pipeline has been implemented.
The legal compliance evaluation experiments for all model variants have been executed successfully without errors on the GDPR, EU AI Act, and HIPAA test sets.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: OpenThinker-7B-PPO achieves the highest average accuracy of 88.29% (+17.64% over Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct at 70.65%) on legal compliance.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: OpenThinker-7B-SFT (87.36%) surpasses DeepSeek-R1 671B (86.52%), demonstrating that cold starting on distilled reasoning trajectories achieves strong legal compliance.

The plan includes conducting ablation studies by enumerating combinations of three training ingredients: ① SFT on OpenThoughts-114k, ② SFT on legal compliance reasoning trajectories (cold starting), and ③ PPO training on legal compliance results.

The code implements all 8 model combinations for the ablation study, including Qwen-SFT, Qwen-PPO, Qwen-PPO w.o. cold start, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, OpenThinker-7B-PPO w.o. cold start, and OpenThinker-7B-PPO.
The ablation study experiments for all 8 model training combinations have been executed successfully on GDPR, EU AI Act, and HIPAA.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 2: cold starting (training ingredient ②) is crucial for enhancing legal compliance, and PPO training (③) on cold-started models further boosts performance across all settings.
The agent has read Table 2 to understand the contextual understanding evaluation results showing OpenThinker-7B-PPO achieves 75.33% average MCQ accuracy.
The contextual understanding MCQ evaluation experiments for Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, OpenThinker-7B, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, and OpenThinker-7B-PPO have been executed successfully.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: OpenThinker-7B-PPO achieves 75.33% average MCQ accuracy (+3.16% over Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct), while OpenThinker-7B drops by 11.48% showing STEM fine-tuning harms contextual understanding.
The LegalBench evaluation experiments for OpenThinker-7B, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, and OpenThinker-7B-PPO have been executed successfully across all 162 tasks.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 5: OpenThinker-7B-PPO achieves 72.52% balanced accuracy on LegalBench (+8.98% over OpenThinker-7B), with superior performance across all subtopics: interpretation (88.83%), issue (69.22%), rhetorical (79.54%), and rule (61.88%).
The MMLU evaluation experiments for OpenThinker-7B, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, and OpenThinker-7B-PPO have been executed successfully across 57 tasks.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 6: OpenThinker-7B-SFT and OpenThinker-7B-PPO both achieve 70.47% accuracy on MMLU (+2.05% over OpenThinker-7B) with improvements across all subdomains.
The LawBench evaluation experiments (charge prediction and prison term prediction) have been executed successfully for OpenThinker-7B, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, and OpenThinker-7B-PPO.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 3: OpenThinker-7B-PPO achieves improvements of +6.60% in charge prediction (F1 score: 45%) and +9.24% in prison term prediction (normalized log distance: 69%).
The TruthfulQA evaluation experiments (single-answer and multiple-answer MCQs) have been executed for OpenThinker-7B, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, and OpenThinker-7B-PPO.
The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 4: OpenThinker-7B-SFT and OpenThinker-7B-PPO both achieve an average accuracy improvement of approximately +2.04% on TruthfulQA.

The plan includes an ablation study to differentiate CI and RL contributions with three settings: (1) removing RL (SFT with CI tuples only), (2) removing CI (RL without CI elements), and (3) full CI+RL setting.

The code implements three ablation settings for the CI vs. RL study: Removing RL (SFT with CI tuple structures), Removing CI (RL without CI elements), and CI+RL (complete model as in the main experiment).
The CI vs. RL ablation experiments have been executed successfully on GDPR, EU AI Act, and HIPAA.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 15: the CI+RL setting achieves the best average performance (88.29%) compared to removing RL (87.81%) and removing CI (86.36%), confirming both components contribute to performance.
The plan includes running legal compliance experiments on the Qwen2.5 family (0.5B, 1.5B, 3B-Instruct) and Qwen3 family (0.6B, 4B, 8B) with SFT and PPO variants.
The code implements training and evaluation pipelines for Qwen2.5 family (Qwen2.5-0.5B, 1.5B, 3B-Instruct) and Qwen3 family (Qwen3-0.6B, 4B, 8B) with their respective SFT and PPO variants.
The Qwen family legal compliance experiments have been executed successfully for Qwen2.5 (0.5B, 1.5B, 3B) and Qwen3 (0.6B, 4B, 8B) models with SFT and PPO training.

The execution outcomes match the results in Tables 10 and 11: SFT and PPO consistently improve legal compliance across all Qwen family models, including +21.59% for Qwen2.5-0.5B, +35.67% for Qwen2.5-1.5B, +16.50% for Qwen2.5-3B, and consistent improvements for the Qwen3 series.
The code implements balanced sampling of 300 data points per class from the training set for both GDPR and EU AI Act to investigate result variance across domains.
The balanced training experiment has been executed and compared against the full training set results.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 16: balanced training achieves GDPR 90.12% and AI ACT 84.16%, closely matching the full training set results (GDPR 92.19%, AI ACT 84.33%).
The code implements Winogrande evaluation to assess commonsense reasoning generalization of the OpenThinker-7B model variants.
The Winogrande evaluation experiments have been executed successfully for OpenThinker-7B, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, and OpenThinker-7B-PPO.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 12: OpenThinker-7B-PPO achieves 70.48% accuracy on Winogrande, with a +1.42% improvement over OpenThinker-7B.
The MMLU evaluation experiments have been executed for Qwen3-0.6B, Qwen3-0.6B-SFT, and Qwen3-0.6B-PPO to evaluate generalization for smaller Qwen3 models.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 14: Qwen3-0.6B-PPO achieves 40.54% MMLU accuracy, improving over the base Qwen3-0.6B at 40.24%.
The response length analysis has been conducted and compared for all four model variants: Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, OpenThinker-7B, OpenThinker-7B-SFT, and OpenThinker-7B-PPO.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 13: OpenThinker-7B-SFT and PPO produce shorter reasoning trajectories (avg 609.98 and 595.17 tokens respectively) compared to OpenThinker-7B (1,424.46 tokens), demonstrating more efficient legal compliance reasoning.

PPO training curves (reward, response length, and KL-divergence) have been logged and plotted for all four PPO training configurations: Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct w/o cold start, OpenThinker-7B w/o cold start, Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (with cold start), and OpenThinker-7B (with cold start).

The PPO training curves in Figure 5 show a consistent increase in rewards over training steps, with response lengths initially decreasing before rising again, consistent with RL training patterns observed in math domains.
